name: Bhairava

visual_attributes.morphology: Anthropomorphic form with multiple arms and a fierce expression
distinctive_features: Accompanied by a dog as a vehicle
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Larger than life-size, often depicted as towering over other figures
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Multiple arms arranged symmetrically, often in a dynamic pose

domain_specific_functional.SHAIVISM_ROLE: Manifestation of Shiva representing Supreme Reality

relationships.description: Bhairava is a powerful manifestation of Shiva in Shaivism.
In Vajrayana Buddhism, Bhairava is considered a fierce emanation of bodhisattva Mañjuśrī.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: India, Nepal, Indonesia, Sri Lanka, Japan, Tibet
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the destruction of fear and protection against evil

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic Hindu deity without distinctive Shaivite features
NOT depicted without a dog or danda
NOT framed solely within a single religious tradition, as Bhairava spans both Hinduism and Buddhism
